export_view.value:
Purchase Orders (POs) are a key component in the procurement process, allowing users to create a fulfillment record of the products they purchase from external suppliers. Working alongside RepairQ's reporting and inventory management features, you can create POs to track spending on ordered products and parts and replenish onhand quantities for accurate inventory management. Users on our Advanced and Professional plans with multiple locations or a dedicated warehouse can also submit store-to-store inventory requests through an Internal Purchase Order (inventory transfer).

https://youtu.be/azhBHGEx3lc

Purchase Order Statuses

Similar to Tickets, Purchase Orders have different statuses to indicate the progression of the order flow.

  • New/Draft - This is a new PO that has saved progress for later attention.

  • Ordered - All items have been added to the PO, and the order has been placed with the supplier. Reorder Points (ROP) will now adjust for the items you have ordered, and you can view the quantity of the ordered items in your Inventory Summary. Once a PO is in Ordered status, you can receive items into your inventory.

  • Partially Shipped - This status is for an internal transfer that has been fulfilled in part by the shipping location.

  • Shipped - This status is for an integrated or internal order that is currently in transit from the shipping location.

  • Partially Received - This is for POs that have received part of the order into inventory. This can be from saving your current progress or if you are waiting for another shipment for the other items.

  • Received - All items on the PO have been received into inventory, but the PO has not been officially closed out.

  • Reconciled - This status exists to close the order from further editing. An order does not require all items to be received before reconciliation. This status is used to note that you have received all available items and are finished with the receiving process. Any unreceived items will be noted as a variance when viewing purchase order details. All information has been finalized for reporting.
